query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
I have completed the research. Here is the key finding:

**Uganda's confirmed Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) case count has remained at 20 and has NOT risen above 20.** No new confirmed cases were reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026.

Cross-validated across all requested sources:
- **ECDC** (page updated 9 July 2026, CDTR Week 27 window): 20 confirmed cases, 2 deaths — explicitly states "the last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then."
- **WHO DON612** (early July): 20 Uganda confirmed cases, 2 deaths (within a total of 1,481 confirmed cases including 1,460 in DRC).
- **WHO DON / ReliefWeb (3 July)** and **Outbreak News Today (8 July)**: 20 confirmed + 1 probable (who died) = 21 combined total, 3 deaths including the probable. Last confirmed case = a truck driver on the DRC–Uganda route, 21 June; 16 recovered.

All outbreak growth is on the DRC side (~1,759 confirmed / 600 deaths as of 8 July 2026).

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 9 July 2026... Uganda: a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths... the last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (WHO DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from DRC, 20 from Uganda and one from France; and 454 deaths including two from Uganda.

sources.title: Disease Outbreak News: Ebola caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (3 July 2026)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, as well as one probable case who has died.

sources.title: Congo Ebola outbreak: Ituri province accounts for nine out of 10 cases; Uganda reports no new cases in 2 weeks
sources.snippet: Uganda 20 confirmed + 1 probable = 21 total; last confirmed case (truck driver) reported 21 June 2026; no new cases in two weeks; 16 recovered, 2 hospitalized.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared 16 May 2026; ~600 confirmed deaths in DRC and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (WHO DON608, ~19 June 2026)
sources.snippet: In total, 915 confirmed cases; 896 from DRC and 19 from Uganda; and 234 deaths including two from Uganda.
